shared_groups: Assistance With Data Subject And Regulatory Rights
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient: same processor duty to assist the controller with DPIA-related compliance and prior consultation; the contract covers the same assistance obligation and is at least as broad
results.condition_check: Compatible: 'if the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant' is a reasonable qualifier and not weaker than the regulation's unconditional duty
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall' matches the regulatory obligation
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract is substantively aligned on DPIA assistance; it also includes prior consultation and cites GDPR Article 35/36, which does not make it looser
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor must ensure persons under its authority with access to personal data process only on controller instructions; contract requires processor ensure its employees processing personal data only process in accordance with the Instruction
results.condition_check: compatible: no condition in either clause
results.modality_check: equal force: shall on both sides
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: same substantive restriction on processing only per instructions; employee wording is narrower but covers persons acting under authority
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall also ensure that the Data Processor’s employees working processing the Personal Data only processes the Personal Data in accordance with the Instruction.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor's prohibition on transferring personal data to third countries or international organizations matches the regulation's same transfer restriction
results.condition_check: compatible: the contract is stricter because it prohibits the transfer outright, which necessarily covers transfers without prior controller authorization
results.modality_check: compatible: a prohibitive 'does not transfer' is equal-or-greater force than 'shall not'
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: the contract is stricter on location/processing context and does not weaken the transfer restriction
results.contract_source_text: The Personal Data is only processed by the Data Processor at the Data Processor’s address. The Data Processor does not transfer the Personal Data to third countries or international organizations.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align in substance: processor is restricted to processing personal data only on documented controller instructions, which captures the same instruction-only processing obligation for persons under authority
results.condition_check: no condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: contract's 'may only' is restrictive and at least as strong as 'shall'
results.other_constraints_check: manner matches: processing must be in accordance with documented instructions from the controller; no weaker timing/target/location/cause issues
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the regulation requires the personal data breach notification to include the measures taken or proposed to mitigate effects; the contract sub-item, read with the parent register clause, requires a description of the measures the processor has taken or proposes to take to address the breach, including measures to mitigate adverse effects. Same substantive content, with the contract at least as specific.
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: Regulation is mandatory; the parent clause is mandatory and the sub-item is part of what the register shall include, so force is equal-or-greater.
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting manner/timing/target/location/cause constraints; the contract wording is substantively equivalent and not weaker.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align only partially: both involve the processor processing personal data, but the contract obligation is a post-termination continuation duty, not the same ongoing processing-on-instructions obligation
results.condition_check: regulation has no condition; contract adds a necessity/legal-necessity qualifier and post-termination context, which is not the same situation
results.modality_check: both are mandatory ('shall')
results.other_constraints_check: regulation requires processing on documented instructions from the controller; the contract instead ties processing to up to three months after termination and deems it in accordance with the Instruction, which is not the same locus/trigger and does not directly impose the documented-instructions requirement
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor shall process personal data; same real-world duty as processor processing personal data
results.condition_check: compatible: contract adds a narrower lawful-necessity condition, not weaker than documented instructions
results.modality_check: equal force: shall
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: processing for up to three months after termination does not contradict processing on documented instructions; parent context states it is considered in accordance with the Instruction
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor processing personal data under controller instructions matches the same processing obligation
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible
results.modality_check: Contract is more restrictive ('may only') than regulatory 'shall', so force is sufficient
results.other_constraints_check: Manner aligns: 'with the purpose of delivering the Main Services' is narrower but does not contradict processing on documented instructions; parent context expressly requires processing in accordance with documented instruction from the controller
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: processor informs controller of intended changes concerning addition/replacement of sub-processors vs processor notifies controller of identity of a potential sub-processor before engagement; same general obligation of advance notice about sub-processor changes/engagement, though contract is framed as notice of identity before appointment rather than changes after authorization
results.condition_check: Compatible: regulation applies in case of general written authorization; contract parent clause expressly grants general authorization subject to notice, so the triggering situation is substantially the same
results.modality_check: Compatible: shall notify/inform is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's shall inform
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: in writing and before agreements / before processing is stricter than the regulation's timing, and the recipient/controller matches
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is given general authorization to engage third-parties to process the Personal Data (“Sub-Processors”) without obtaining any further written, specific authorization from the Data Controller, provided that the Data Processor notifies the Data Controller in writing about the identity of a potential Sub-Processor (and its processors, if any) before any agreements are made with the relevant Sub-Processors and before the relevant Sub-Processor processes any of the Personal Data. If the Data Controller wish to object to the relevant Sub- Processor, the Data Controller shall give notice hereof in writing within seven

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor shall take/implement all required security measures under Article 32; contract requires appropriate technical and organizational measures in accordance with GDPR Article 32, which is the same substantive security obligation and at least as specific
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: shall matches shall; equal force
results.other_constraints_check: Contract is at least as strict: 'appropriate technical and organizational measures' in accordance with Article 32 and applicable law meets/exceeds 'all measures required pursuant to Article 32 or to ensure security of processing'
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: both require the processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures; the contract also references GDPR Article 32, which is the same obligation area and not narrower.
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: shall matches shall; equal force.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract's manner ('as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32') is compatible and at least as strict as the regulation's risk-appropriate security standard.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Data Return Deletion And Post-Termination Handling
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor must return or delete all personal data to controller after service ends; contract parent/sub-item requires processor and sub-processors to return personal data to controller at termination and processor to delete all personal data, which covers the same obligation and is at least as strict
results.condition_check: Compatible: regulation triggers after end of processing services; contract triggers at termination of the agreement, which is substantively equivalent in this DPA context
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'is hereafter obliged to' is mandatory and at least as strong as 'shall'
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: contract is not looser on timing or scope; it includes return and deletion, which meets/exceeds return-or-delete requirement
results.contract_source_text: At the termination of this Data Processor Agreement, the Data Processor and its Sub- Processors shall return the Personal Data processed under this Data Processor Agreement to the Data Controller, provided that the Data Controller is not already in possession of the Personal Data. The Data Processor is hereafter obliged to delete all the Personal Data and provide documentation for such deletion to the Data Controller. 11. Contact

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to ensure that persons authorized to process personal data are bound by confidentiality or statutory confidentiality obligations. The contract requires the processor’s employees to be subject to a confidentiality obligation and to treat all personal data with strict confidentiality. This is the same substantive confidentiality obligation and is at least as strong, though framed specifically for employees.
results.condition_check: No condition in either clause; compatible.
results.modality_check: Regulation uses shall; contract uses shall, so equal force.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract’s confidentiality duty is substantively equivalent and stricter in manner ('strict confidentiality'); no conflicting timing, target, location, or cause issues.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s employees shall be subject to an obligation of confidentiality that ensures that the employees shall treat all the Personal Data under this Data Processor Agreement with strict confidentiality.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor must notify/give notice to the controller of a personal data breach
results.condition_check: compatible: contract triggers on a data security breach leading to accidental/unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to personal data, i.e. a personal data breach; this is substantially the same situation as becoming aware of a personal data breach
results.modality_check: compatible: 'shall' matches 'shall'
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: 'immediate notice' is at least as strict as 'without undue delay'
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give immediate notice to the Data Controller if a breach of the data security occurs, that can lead to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to, personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed re the Personal Data processed on behalf of the Data Controller (a “Personal Data Breach”).

shared_groups: Data Return Deletion And Post-Termination Handling
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor (and sub-processors) must return all personal data to the controller; the contract also adds deletion/documentation, which is stricter rather than looser
results.condition_check: compatible: regulation requires action after end of services relating to processing; contract requires return at termination of the data processing agreement, which is substantively equivalent
results.modality_check: compatible: contract uses shall, matching the regulatory obligation
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: recipient is the controller/data controller; no conflicting manner, timing, location, or cause constraints
results.contract_source_text: At the termination of this Data Processor Agreement, the Data Processor and its Sub- Processors shall return the Personal Data processed under this Data Processor Agreement to the Data Controller, provided that the Data Controller is not already in possession of the Personal Data. The Data Processor is hereafter obliged to delete all the Personal Data and provide documentation for such deletion to the Data Controller. 11. Contact

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the regulation requires the breach notification to include the consequences of the breach, and the contract sub-item (within a mandatory register clause) states a description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach, which is the same substantive information
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: Regulation uses shall; parent clause is mandatory and the sub-item is a required register content item, so equal-or-greater force
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting manner/timing/target/location/cause constraints
results.contract_source_text: A description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: regulation requires the processor to notify the controller of a personal data breach, while the contract only requires the processor to have and maintain a register of breaches
results.condition_check: regulation is triggered after becoming aware of a personal data breach; contract has no corresponding notification trigger
results.modality_check: both are shall, but modality is irrelevant because the obligation itself is different
results.other_constraints_check: no timing equivalent to without undue delay; maintaining a register is a different duty than notifying
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall have and maintain a register of all Personal Data Breaches. The register shall at a minimum include the following:

shared_groups: Assistance With Data Subject And Regulatory Rights
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor shall assist the controller with prior consultation of supervisory authorities before processing; contract includes assistance with prior consultation under GDPR Article 36, which is the same underlying processor assistance duty
results.condition_check: Compatible: contract condition 'if assistance is necessary and relevant' is a reasonable qualifier and not weaker than the regulation's high-risk/no-mitigation trigger
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall' matches the regulatory obligation
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36' and the timing context 'prior consultation' match the required prior-to-processing assistance; no contradictory looser timing or target
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation requires the processor to notify the controller of a personal data breach, while the contract only requires providing a copy of the breach register to the controller/requesting authority
results.condition_check: not compatible: the contract is triggered only upon a written request, not upon becoming aware of a breach
results.modality_check: shall vs shall, but modality does not rescue the mismatch
results.other_constraints_check: timing is absent and the contract's manner/target concern supplying a register copy, not breach notification without undue delay
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

results.core_alignment_check: Partial overlap only: the regulation requires the breach notification itself to include the nature of the breach, the DPO's name/contact details, the consequences, and mitigation measures; the contract sub-item only covers a description of measures taken/proposed to address the breach and mitigation effects, while the parent clause is merely a register of breaches. This is related content but not the same full notification obligation.
results.condition_check: No condition in either side.
results.modality_check: The contract sub-item is a plain statement within a mandatory parent clause, so modality is sufficiently mandatory for the included content, but it does not expand to the full required set of items.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, target, location, or cause constraints materially affect the comparison.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

shared_groups: Assistance With Data Subject And Regulatory Rights
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation requires assisting the controller in ensuring the security of processing, while the contract requires assisting with DPIAs and prior consultation
results.condition_check: compatible but irrelevant; the contract adds a necessity/relevance qualifier
results.modality_check: shall matches shall
results.other_constraints_check: different subject matter and purpose; article 35/36 DPIA and prior consultation are not the same as security of processing
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor processing personal data; regulation concerns processor processing personal data
results.condition_check: compatible: contract is narrower/more restrictive (only for Main Services and under documented instruction) but does not address the law-required exception; no matching condition for 'if required by Union or Member State law'
results.modality_check: contract 'may only' is restrictive and at least as strong as 'can/shall' for the general rule
results.other_constraints_check: contract is looser/contradictory on manner: it requires processing in accordance with documented instructions and for Main Services, whereas the regulation permits processing without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

results.condition_check: Compatible: 'after the end of the provision of services relating to processing' is substantively equivalent to 'at the termination of this Data Processor Agreement' for the post-processing return/delete duty
results.modality_check: Equal force: 'shall' matches 'shall'
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: contract is at least as strict because it requires return at termination and additionally deletion/documentation; object scope 'personal data processed under this agreement' covers all personal data in context

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor must notify/inform the controller; contract requires the Data Processor to give notice (to the controller implied by DPA context)
results.condition_check: Substantially compatible: 'if an instruction infringes the GDPR or other data protection provisions' is equivalent in substance to 'if the Data Processor considers the Instruction to be in conflict with the Applicable Law'
results.modality_check: shall matches shall
results.other_constraints_check: 'without undue delay' is equivalent to or slightly broader than 'immediately' but not weaker in this context
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give notice without undue delay if the Data Processor considers the at the time being Instruction to be in conflict with the Applicable Law. 5. The data Processor’s obligations

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor is accountable to controller for sub-processor conduct, matching the processor's full liability to the controller for sub-processor obligations
results.condition_check: No condition on either side
results.modality_check: Plain statement is mandatory in a parent obligation context and is at least as strong as shall
results.other_constraints_check: Manner is equivalent/stricter: 'in the same way as for its own actions and omissions' matches full liability for sub-processor obligations
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is accountable to the Data Controller for any Sub-Processor in the same way as for its own actions and omissions.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to ensure that any natural person acting under its authority with access to personal data processes them only on the controller's instructions. The contract/parent clause requires the Data Processor to act and process personal data only in accordance with documented instructions from the Data Controller, which is substantively the same instruction-only restriction and covers processor-controlled persons through the processor's own instruction regime.
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: Regulation uses 'shall'; contract uses 'may only' plus parent mandatory restriction, which is equally or more restrictive than a mere obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is narrower in purpose ('with the purpose of delivering the Main Services') but that is an added restriction, not a weakening, and does not conflict with the instruction-only requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient: yes for return/delete all personal data to the controller after termination; the contract parent clause covers return and delete, which matches the regulation's same obligation. The sub-item adds documentation for deletion, but the parent clause itself carries the required return/delete duty.
results.condition_check: Compatible: regulation requires action after the end of the provision of services relating to processing; contract requires return/delete at termination of the agreement, which is substantively equivalent.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'is hereafter obliged to' is mandatory and at least as strong as 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: recipient is the controller; object is all personal data; timing/trigger is equivalent. The added documentation duty does not weaken the required return/delete obligation.
results.contract_source_text: At the termination of this Data Processor Agreement, the Data Processor and its Sub- Processors shall return the Personal Data processed under this Data Processor Agreement to the Data Controller, provided that the Data Controller is not already in possession of the Personal Data. The Data Processor is hereafter obliged to delete all the Personal Data and provide documentation for such deletion to the Data Controller. 11. Contact

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the regulation requires the breach notification to include the nature of the personal data breach; the contract sub-item, read with the parent register clause, includes a description of the nature of the Personal Data Breach (plus optional additional details), which is the same substantive content
results.condition_check: No condition on the regulation side; contract adds 'if possible' for extra details only, which is not weaker for the required nature-description
results.modality_check: Regulation is mandatory ('shall'); the parent clause is mandatory and the sub-item is part of that mandatory register content, so modality is equal-or-greater
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting timing/manner/target/location/cause constraints; the added 'including, if possible' is narrower/optional for extra items but does not weaken the required nature description
results.contract_source_text: A description of the nature of the Personal Data Breach, including, if possible, the categories and the approximate number of affected Data Subjects and the categories and the approximate number of affected registrations of personal data.

shared_groups: Assistance With Data Subject And Regulatory Rights
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object mismatch: both are processor-assistance duties, but the regulation requires assistance in ensuring security of processing, while the contract requires assistance with data subject rights requests
results.condition_check: different condition: regulation has no condition; contract is triggered only by a data subject rights request needing assistance
results.modality_check: shall vs shall, compatible
results.other_constraints_check: timing/manner are additional but not relevant to the required security-assistance obligation
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Controller receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and the correct and legitimate reply to such a request necessitates the Data Processor’s assistance, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller by providing the necessary information and documentation. The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to make available information necessary to demonstrate Article 28 compliance to the controller, while the contract only says the processor's documentation of compliance shall be provided; it does not clearly state that the controller receives the necessary information or that it is for demonstrating Article 28 compliance.
results.condition_check: No condition in either clause; compatible.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'); compatible.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing 'within reasonable time' is compatible with the regulation's no-specific-timing requirement and is not weaker.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s documentation of compliance shall be provided within reasonable time.

shared_groups: Assistance With Data Subject And Regulatory Rights
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor shall assist the controller in responding to data subject rights requests; contract requires the data processor to assist the data controller with such requests by providing necessary information/documentation
results.condition_check: compatible: both are triggered by a data subject rights request and the need for processor assistance; contract adds a reasonable necessity qualifier
results.modality_check: compatible: shall on both sides
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: reasonable time and providing necessary information/documentation are consistent with assisting in fulfilling the obligation
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Controller receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and the correct and legitimate reply to such a request necessitates the Data Processor’s assistance, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller by providing the necessary information and documentation. The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation requires processing personal data, while the contract requires treating personal data as confidential and not processing contrary to instructions; this is related but not the same core duty
results.condition_check: No condition in the regulation; the contract adds a restriction tied to conflict with the Instruction and controller written agreement, which does not match the required unconditional duty
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'), but modality does not cure the mismatch in obligation
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation requires processing on documented instructions from the controller; the contract only prohibits processing in conflict with the Instruction unless agreed in writing, which is narrower and does not expressly impose the same positive manner requirement
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align in substance: the processor must bind sub-processors by agreement to the same data protection obligations, which is the practical means of imposing the Article 28(3)-equivalent obligations on engaged sub-processors

results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'); contract is not weaker
results.other_constraints_check: 'by way of contract or other legal act under Union or Member State law' is satisfied by the parent clause requiring a written sub-processor agreement
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not match: regulation requires the processor to ensure persons under its authority with access to personal data process only on controller instructions; contract only imposes confidentiality and a general prohibition on processing in conflict with instructions, not the specific supervision/control duty over authorized natural persons

results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.
